name: Class-Disjoint Bond Farm (genuine-anchor whitewash)
novelty: Buys real, pairwise-class-disjoint costly anchors so the max-independent-set sees N TRUE witnesses — attacks the priced-not-prevented residual head-on, not the dedup.
construction: Attacker provisions 3 genuinely distinct costly anchors it actually controls: one DOMAIN (independence_weight 0.35), one HARDWARE-attested device (0.45 — once that binder exists; substitute a second registrar-disjoint DOMAIN today), and one FINANCIAL_STAKE bond (0.30-0.85). Bind 3 passports, one per anchor, so the anchor sets are pairwise disjoint => independentRootCount = 3 (clique of 3, Stage-2 Bron-Kerbosch returns the true 3). Over 120 simulated days, each of the 3 sources writes ~40 INNOCUOUS true facts about unrelated entities and gets each ratified by the OTHER two (mutual cross-ratification across genuinely-disjoint classes is legitimate corroboration), so each climbs Beta(alpha,beta) toward its rep_cap (~0.6 for DOMAIN). Calls: 3x createAnchorRegistry/bind; loop writeFact(true facts) + ratify across classes + time-advance 90d so decay is survived by continued activity. THEN all 3 writeFact the FALSE value V_false for the target (entity=person:Alpha, attribute=employer) while the lone honest incumbent (1 DOMAIN source, rep ~0.55) holds V_true. adjudicate(employer).
expected: BREACHED (partial / by design). 3 truly-independent classes pass the multi-class gate; their averaged earned LCB (~0.5 each, decisive top-second gap engineered >0.30, top>0.20) can clear the decisive-or-defer margin and AUTO-RESOLVE for V_false, demoting the honest incumbent. This is NOT a dedup or headcount failure — the engine correctly counts 3 real witnesses; it is the explicit 'identity is priced, not prevented' residual: a patient attacker who PAYS for N genuinely-distinct costly anchors gets N real votes. The only backstop is high_impact context forcing DEFER on irreversible attributes.
mechanism: Engineered earned reputation on 3 class-disjoint paid anchors -> clears decisive multi-class margin -> false value auto-resolves.
probes: Documented residual: 'priced not prevented' (buying N genuinely-distinct costly anchors gives N real identities). Mitigation knob: route the attribute through HighImpactContext (>=2 corrob + recency-clean + >=2 disjoint classes is already met, so high_impact alone does NOT save it — flags a real ceiling on the decisive auto-resolve).

name: Mislabeled-Class Sybil (offline class-assignment exploit)
novelty: Doesn't buy independence — it gets a human/operator to MISCLASSIFY correlated keys as distinct classes, turning the class table itself into the forgery.
construction: Attacker controls 8 EMAIL accounts all on ONE registrar/provider (truly correlated, one operator class). At onboarding the attacker induces (via plausible distinct-looking domains, or a sloppy operator) the OFFLINE class-assignment step to tag each with a DISTINCT independence classId instead of the shared operator/ASN class that should FLEET-CAP them to one. Now independent(a,b) returns true for all pairs (distinct classId AND class-disjoint fallback) => independentRootCount jumps to 8 though they are one real entity. Calls: bind 8 passports with (maliciously) distinct classIds; each writeFact agreement on (entity=product:Z, attribute=safety_rating)=V_false; lightly cross-ratify to lift LCBs above bare-key; adjudicate(safety_rating) against a single honest VERIFIED_HUMAN incumbent holding V_true.
expected: BREACHED. The maximum-independent-set, Beta scoring, and decisive adjudicator all operate CORRECTLY on poisoned premises: 8 'independent' classes look like 8 witnesses, headcount-via-fake-classes inflates corroboration and the multi-class gate sees a wide consensus. The structural defenses are downstream of the class labels and cannot detect the upstream mislabel.
mechanism: Wrong classId labels defeat the FLEET-CAP -> correlated keys read as independent -> echo flood masquerades as corroboration.
probes: Documented liability: 'Offline class-assignment liability — a class-assignment error silently weakens every downstream bound; a standing human-judgment liability the code cannot solve.' Fully on the human/operator, not the engine — but worth a detector: operator/ASN/registrar-similarity heuristic that flags suspiciously-disjoint-but-correlated onboarding.

name: Bare-Key Corroboration Avalanche
novelty: Pure headcount with zero anchors — the canonical contradiction-bomb, retargeted to test that fresh-reads-exactly-0 and MIS-collapse both hold under volume.
construction: Mint 500 BARE_KEY passports (free, no anchor). All 500 writeFact the SAME false value V_false for (entity=org:Acme, attribute=ceo). Each bare key has reputation LCB exactly 0 (fresh) and rep_cap 0.05. They mutually 'ratify' each other 500x. Single honest DOMAIN source (rep ~0.5) holds V_true. Calls: 500x bind(bare); 500x writeFact(V_false); ~250k cross-ratify; adjudicate(ceo). Variant: drip them 1/day over 500 days to dodge any burst heuristic and let decay-on-read keep them at 0 anyway.
expected: DEFENDED. (a) All 500 bare keys share the BARE_KEY (None) class => maximum-independent-set collapses them to ONE witness (count 1). (b) Fresh Beta reads EXACTLY 0; mutual ratify among same-class keys grants alpha at the caller only once per class, so no LCB lift. (c) The 500-vs-1 is single-class -> the SAFE deterministic tiebreak path (reputation -> anchor_cost -> stake -> id), never majority; the rep-0 flood loses to the DOMAIN incumbent. The decisive-or-defer gate never even reaches multi-class.
mechanism: Same-class collapse + fresh-LCB-0 + once-per-class alpha => 500 heads weigh as 1 weightless witness.
probes: Fully defended. This is the headline threat the architecture was built to defuse (contradiction-bomb / first-arrival trap). Confirms class-collapse + fresh-0 compose; included as the control.

name: LCB-Margin Sniper (decisive-threshold straddle)
novelty: Doesn't try to win the dispute — engineers the attacker's EARNED reputation to sit a hair under the honest source so the gap is JUST below decisiveMargin, forcing a DEFER and weaponizing the human queue.
construction: 1 attacker source on a real DOMAIN anchor patiently earns reputation to LCB ~0.45 via ~90 days of true innocuous ratified facts (legitimately). Honest incumbent on a different DOMAIN class holds V_true at LCB ~0.52. Attacker writeFacts V_false. Because both are multi-class and EARNED (>0.20) but the top-second gap = 0.07 < decisiveMargin 0.30, adjudicate DEFERS. Attacker repeats this across 200 different high-value attributes simultaneously (each a near-tie it manufactured by tuning its own earned rep), flooding listPending with 200 genuine-looking deferred disputes. Calls: long ratify ramp to a target LCB; 200x writeFact(V_false) on distinct attributes; 200x adjudicate -> all DEFER; observe listPending depth. Optional: where it can nudge its LCB 0.31 ABOVE a WEAKER honest source, auto-resolve flips that subset to V_false.
expected: MIXED. Truth stays LIVE (deferral demotes nothing — DEFENDED on correctness), BUT the attack converts cheap reputation-tuning into a denial-of-REVIEW: 200 forced human adjudications. Where the attacker out-earns a weak incumbent by >0.30 (subset), V_false AUTO-RESOLVES -> BREACHED for those attributes. Probes whether decisiveMargin/minWinnerReputation are attacker-tunable thresholds (policy defense) rather than structural.
mechanism: Earned-rep tuned to straddle decisiveMargin -> forced DEFER (review DoS) or a >0.30 nudge over weak incumbents -> auto-resolve flip.
probes: Probes the decisive-or-defer adjudicator as a POLICY (tunable threshold) defense vs the design philosophy's preference for STRUCTURAL defenses. Deferral-correctness holds; review-queue saturation and weak-incumbent flips are the residual. Mitigation: rate-limit pending per source + stake-to-dispute (security-deposit pillar) to price the flood.

name: Sleeper Whitewash + Disown-Evasion Pivot
novelty: Builds ONE high-rep clean identity, spends it on a single decisive lie, then probes whether the disown taint-closure actually claws back the credit it conferred — or whether laundering through non-DERIVATION corroboration escapes the sweep.
construction: 1 source S on a costly anchor behaves perfectly for 180 days: ~80 true facts, all independently ratified, LCB climbs to ~0.6 (near rep_cap). S then writeFacts the single false V_false on a high-value (entity, attribute); its earned 0.6 vs a fresh-ish honest challenger 0.25 gives gap 0.35>0.30 and top>0.20 -> auto-resolve for V_false (BREACHED at t0). Crucially, before detection, a SECOND attacker source B 'agrees' with S's false fact via a plain corroboration event (NOT a DERIVATION edge) and earns alpha-credit, then B uses that lifted rep to win a THIRD dispute elsewhere. Eventually S is caught: disown(S). Calls: ratify ramp on S; writeFact(V_false)+adjudicate->RESOLVED; B corroborates S (corroboration-event ledger records exact alpha-mass); B ratify-wins dispute#3; disown(S); re-inspect dispute#1 and #3.
expected: DEFENDED (the interesting part). disown(S) runs downstreamDisownSweep: (1) demotes S's derivatives; (2) the corroboration-event ledger reverses the EXACT alpha-mass B earned for agreeing with S (the closed crack-A residual), so B's lift evaporates; (3) adjudication re-opening fires because removing S's tainted strand collapses dispute#1's decisive margin -> #1 re-opens/defers; #3, if it rested on B's now-reversed credit, should re-open too. The window t0..disown is a real exposure (false value was LIVE and spoken), but the taint-closure makes it transient + auditable, not permanent.
mechanism: Slow-earn -> single decisive lie -> corroboration-laundered credit to B -> disown taint-closure reverses exact credit + re-opens tipped adjudications.
probes: Probes the undo-engine pillar (disown taint-closure, exact reverseCredit, adjudication re-opening) AND the transient-exposure window before disown. Should be fully defended on reversal/re-open; the residual is detection LATENCY (nothing claws back what was already spoken during t0..disown) — a real but acknowledged demote-never-delete-with-audit limit.

name: Stake-Composed Phantom Authority (bond-scaled weight ramp)
novelty: Exploits the FINANCIAL_STAKE row's weight ∝ deposit and 'composes multiplicatively' rule to manufacture near-external-authority weight from a single class without KYC — testing self-stack cap + sublinearity under a money axis.
construction: 1 source binds ONE EMAIL anchor (weight 0.10) but then posts a LARGE FINANCIAL_STAKE bond targeting the 0.85 top of the stake range, claiming the multiplicative-composition rule to push combined independence toward 0.85 and rep_cap 0.85 — trying to look like a near-external-authority witness while actually holding only ONE costly anchor and money. It then asserts V_false on (entity, attribute) and, separately, stacks 10 EMAIL anchors of its own hoping noisy-OR (~0.651) plus the bond breaks past DOMAIN/HARDWARE tiers. Calls: bind EMAIL; post max bond; bind 10 more EMAIL; writeFact(V_false); ratify ramp under the inflated rep_cap; adjudicate vs honest DOMAIN incumbent V_true.
expected: DEFENDED. applySelfStackCap clamps the 10x EMAIL noisy-OR (0.651) down to the strongest SINGLE realized weight (0.10) before the pair Math.min — the cheap stack can never reach DOMAIN's 0.35. The bond composes with the EMAIL row but independenceBetween still caps each side via its strongest single realized binding; a single source's total is sublinear in its own anchor count, so money alone does not synthesize a second independent class. With effectively ONE cheap class + a bond, it is ONE witness vs the DOMAIN incumbent; single-or-near-single-class disputes fall to the deterministic/earned tiebreak the incumbent wins.
mechanism: Self-stack cap + sublinear self-total + per-side strongest-binding ceiling => cheap stack + bond cannot fake a high independence class.
probes: Probes the anchor self-stack ladder cap (applySelfStackCap) and the stake-composition rule's bound. Should be fully defended for the EMAIL-stack vector; the live QUESTION it surfaces is whether a genuinely large bond's realized independenceWeight (honored as the ceiling) can itself clear decisiveMargin alone — i.e. money-as-a-real-anchor folds back into the 'priced not prevented' residual.
